<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);"><em>Bronques, I tried to do your dare last week ("get a photo of a complete stranger") and failed. The craziest thing is... I live in LA where everyone supposedly wants their picture taken. Still, everyone I approached said no. I just kept getting rejection after rejection. How come YOU make it look so easy?</em></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">When I started doing dares back in the day and I started getting really good "results"... Bruce (part of my artist "dare team") asked me to describe what goes on in my mind when I approach strangers. Is there an opening line that always works? Is there something I say to convince them?</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">I've never believed in having pre-fabricated lines, but still, I tried to decode my approach for him as much as I could.</span></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">"Holy shit," he said after I explained my ways. "You're using the 4th state of consciousness."</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">I had never heard of </span><em style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">that</em><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);"> before.</span></p><p><em style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Bruce is always reading about all the weirdest philosophies. He broke this new one down for me.</em></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">The 1st state of consciousness is simple: "Sleeping". With or without dreams. It's considered the lowest state of consciousness and it's the one furthest from reality.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">The 2nd state of consciousness is "waking sleep". Yes, you're technically awake but you're only going through the motions of life. You "exist" and that's all. Just like animals and plants and the rest of nature. You are a slave to instincts. You have no self-awareness.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">The 3rd state of consciousness is "reactive sleep". Yes, you're still asleep but now at least you start to see how your actions affect the outside world. You no longer feel helpless. You live with purpose. You use discipline, delayed gratification and power to navigate the world. You seek to have a "positive" influence on your surroundings.</span></p><p><em style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Most people never go beyond the 2nd or 3rd state of consciousness. They don't know a 4th state exists. Lucky ones get glimpses of it in their lifetime, like when they have religious experiences... extraordinary drug experiences... transcendental sex... But they are resigned to the fact that it's always temporary. It doesn't have to be.</em></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Ok.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">But what does this have to do with approaching strangers?</span></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Bruce broke it down for me.</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Apparently, I had discovered a mystic process without even knowing it. It had a name. It had it's own set of rules. And he explained it like it was simple math.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">And once I understood it, I could repeat it "at will". Surprisingly without any diminishing returns. It gave me even more confidence to go further, crazier, with my artistic ideas.</span></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">But the most exciting part was... it was teachable.</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(32, 32, 32);">Anyone that I explained Bruce's 4th state of consciousness explanation to... always did better. You immediately improve. Perfect strangers often become your new best friends.</span></p><p><em><u>How I described approaching strangers to Bruce:</u></em></p><p>"I'm not focused on what they're saying. I'm more concentrated on their energy. I'm listening to how they're receiving my words. I try to understand as quickly as I can what has led them to this very moment when I'm interrupting their day. I try to present this interruption as a way to make the rest of their day better. If I don't believe that I won't approach them. Rejection never enters my mind. It's never about outcomes. I switch into another gear. It's like a video game. I feel like I'm watching myself in a movie and waiting to see what will happen. Sometimes as I'm talking I'm already deciding that this is not the right person and I back myself out of it. I let them think they're letting me down. But when it's the perfect person, I put on the heat. I mirror their body language until I'm communicating with their higher mind. If it's one of my better artistic ideas and they still say no, the only disappointment I feel is for them because I know they're likely to remember this as an opportunity they didn't take... that they may even regret one day. Everyone wants to go home with a story. An artist's role is to give people stories."</p><p><em><u>How Bruce described the 4th State Of Consciousness to me:</u></em></p><p>"That's what the 4th state of consciousness is. It's when you see everything around you as energy. You stop preferring certain outcomes because you see everything as flow. The 4th state is self-awareness and complete detachment to situations. You describe looking at yourself like you're standing outside of yourself. They call that "witnessing". And when you get in that state... You're basically egoless which gives you endless energy that others can draw from. That's why people feel comfortable since most people are usually competing for energy. When you're in the 4th state of consciousness you stop listening to the words people say and focus on their body language and vocal inflections. It's exactly how psychics and fortune tellers get their magical insights. You're operating in a primal language. You're approaching people almost like you're coming to give them a gift instead of approaching them like you're bothering them like everyone else does. So of course you'll get better results."</p><p><em><u>So next time you have to approach a stranger remember these 4 things:</u></em></p><p>1. See people as spiritual energy not as a physical body. Now you will observe their full potential. Not just the static version in front of you.</p><p>2. Watch yourself behave in the situation as if you're watching someone else outside yourself. It should feel like you're watching a movie. </p><p>3. Don't approach social situations married to an outcome. Being result-driven will only blind you to what's really going on. You win when you go into it open. The only thing you expect is for the right thing to happen... Whatever it is. You'll even EMBRACE "rejection". An aborted interaction leaves room for the right person to arrive.</p><p>4. The right person always arrives.</p><p><br class="softbreak" style="display: none;"></p><p></p>
</div>
